gefcom2012_load.note: 20 utility zones from the GEFCom2012 load track. Too few series to reach a reportable Final-Query sample on its own, so it is supplementary: it joins the pooled roster and the per-cell tables, but no standalone GEFCom claim is reportable. Zones within one utility are also plausibly correlated.

legacy_monash:covid_deaths.note: Init Harness / pre-benchmark exposed corpus. Support-A only by exposure class; never eligible for any Query role.

legacy_monash:fred_md.note: Init Harness / pre-benchmark exposed corpus. Support-A only by exposure class; never eligible for any Query role.

legacy_monash:nn5_daily.note: Init Harness / pre-benchmark exposed corpus. Support-A only by exposure class; never eligible for any Query role.

legacy_monash:saugeenday.note: Init Harness / pre-benchmark exposed corpus. Support-A only by exposure class; never eligible for any Query role.

legacy_monash:sunspot.note: Init Harness / pre-benchmark exposed corpus. Support-A only by exposure class; never eligible for any Query role.

legacy_monash:tourism_monthly.note: Init Harness / pre-benchmark exposed corpus. Support-A only by exposure class; never eligible for any Query role.

legacy_monash:us_births.note: Init Harness / pre-benchmark exposed corpus. Support-A only by exposure class; never eligible for any Query role.

metr_la.note: 207 loop detectors on the Los Angeles freeway graph. Split is atomic at the spatial block (20 blocks over 137 co-location-merged sites), so adjacent sensors cannot straddle Support and Query. Claim is bounded to unseen blocks within this network; cross-network evidence must come from monash:traffic_hourly, which is a different city.

monash:covid_deaths.note: Cumulative national COVID death counts. Near-monotone, tiny seasonal differences, so the sMASE denominator is small and losses live on a much larger numeric scale than the rest of the roster. Per-cell tables carry a scale annotation; do not read its sMASE against other datasets' unscaled.

monash:nn5_daily.note: Daily cash withdrawals at UK ATMs.

monash:traffic_hourly.note: 862 sensors on the San Francisco Bay Area freeway graph. The pinned Monash release ships NO sensor coordinates, so unlike METR-LA this dataset CANNOT be spatially blocked and its split stays per-series. Adjacent-sensor leakage across roles is therefore possible and un-quantified; treat within-dataset numbers as an optimistic bound and rely on METR-LA for the spatially clean traffic read.

noaa_global_hourly.note: NOAA ISD hourly surface temperature, the sealed U (unseen-domain) pool. Stations are geographically dispersed across the US by a hash-ordered selection. Carries heavy, genuinely natural missingness -- which is the point: it is the one source whose defects nobody injected.

uci_electricity_load_diagrams.note: 370 independent Portuguese client meters, 15-minute kW resampled to hourly means.
